verdict: Implementation QA iter2 PASS with warnings. All 4 required target files contain the correct contract language for the provenance split derivation. pytest 5/5 PASS. No TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels. One major warning: dev under-reports files_modified (claims 2, git diff shows 4 actually modified). This is inverse of the over-reporting this spec prevents, and does not affect correctness of the implementations. Minor: ??-prefixed qualifier absent from implementation text (functionally equivalent). No blockers preventing close.

warnings.description: Dev under-reports files_modified: dev report claims 2 modified files (agents/dev.md, agents/changelog-analyst.md) but git diff --name-only 1a1d058 shows 4 files actually modified: agents/dev.md, agents/changelog-analyst.md, agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md. agents/qa.md and commands/dev.md are NOT in baseline_dirty_snapshot and are NOT claimed by any other task in the batch.
warnings.impact: Dev self-report is inaccurate. The missing files (agents/qa.md for AC3, commands/dev.md for AC1) are required by the spec and ARE correctly modified — the implementation is correct, only the self-report is incomplete. This is the INVERSE of the over-reporting bug this spec was designed to prevent, and is not caught by the new provenance check.
warnings.recommendation: Dev agent should re-derive files_modified from 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' as mandated by the new M2 contract it just authored — applying the rule to itself.

warnings.description: ??-prefixed qualifier absent from implementation text: M2 and M5 require 'EXCLUDING paths that appeared as untracked entries (??-prefixed lines) in the parsed baseline_dirty_snapshot'; agents/dev.md line 495 says 'EXCLUDING any paths that appear in baseline_dirty_snapshot' (no ??-prefixed qualifier); agents/changelog-analyst.md line 127 says 'absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot' (no ??-prefixed qualifier). AC2 THEN and AC4 THEN clauses also don't require the ??-prefixed qualifier, so tests pass.
warnings.impact: Functionally equivalent: modified-tracked files (M, A, D prefix in baseline_dirty_snapshot) cannot appear in git ls-files --others --exclude-standard output, so the broader 'appear in baseline_dirty_snapshot' filter produces identical results. Non-blocking.
warnings.recommendation: Future BA cycle could tighten AC THEN clauses to require ??-prefixed qualifier for precision. Not required for this cycle's close.

all_findings.issue: dev.files_modified lists only 2 files; git diff --name-only 1a1d058 shows 4 files modified (agents/dev.md, agents/changelog-analyst.md, agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md). agents/qa.md and commands/dev.md are missing from the self-report.
all_findings.recommendation: Dev should apply the new M2 derivation rule to its own report: use git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> to derive files_modified.

all_findings.issue: ??-prefixed qualifier absent from implementation prose. M2/M5 require it; AC THEN clauses don't; implementation uses broader form. Functionally equivalent.
all_findings.recommendation: No action required this cycle. Future BA cycle may tighten AC THEN clauses.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The root cause (dev self-reporting files already in git HEAD as modified/created) is addressed by the four-part contract: (1) commands/dev.md now captures baseline before dispatch; (2) agents/dev.md now mandates diff-derived file lists; (3) agents/qa.md now fails cycles where dev.files_modified/created don't match the diff; (4) agents/changelog-analyst.md now excludes anomaly paths from enrichment. The split derivation (ls-files for files_created) ensures new untracked files are correctly distinguished from modified tracked files.

baseline_dirty_snapshot_entries: agents/dev.md is NOT in dirty snapshot
agents/changelog-analyst.md is NOT in dirty snapshot
agents/qa.md is NOT in dirty snapshot
commands/dev.md is NOT in dirty snapshot
provenance_check.under_reporting_observation: agents/qa.md and commands/dev.md appear in git diff but not in dev.files_modified — under-reporting (W1 warning above)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex examined stale qa-report-20260522-080646-D.json (prior iteration, status fail) and surfaced 3 findings relevant to earlier iterations: (1) M5 ..HEAD prohibition — now confirmed resolved in iter3; (2) QA files_created gap — now confirmed resolved in agents/qa.md lines 425-438; (3) AC4 test semantic detection — test uses per-line regex which is the correct approach. Codex also independently flagged the dev provenance under-reporting (2 vs 4 files), which is the W1 major warning above. No Codex finding requires changing the PASS verdict.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: W1 (dev under-reporting) added to warnings as major finding based on Codex confirmation. All other Codex findings traced to stale iter1/iter2 data; not applicable to iter3 state.
